title: Shared Memory, Mutexes, Atomics, the Memory Model, and Race Evidence · Language Lens: Once Cond Coordination
summary: Concurrent workers update shared counters, caches, and snapshots; plausible output hides races and broken publication. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go once cond coordination increment in a race-free shared-state component. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use Once, OnceValue, Cond, and channel alternatives only when their wakeup, predicate-loop, and failure semantics fit.
Protect one documented invariant with Mutex or RWMutex while minimizing lock scope and avoiding copy or reentrancy errors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
